root.short: Shell-level AI provider isolation for Claude Code
root.long:
cc-select lets different terminal windows on the same machine use different AI model providers.

Unlike cc-switch (global switch), cc-select exports environment variables in the current shell, affecting only the current terminal and its child processes, achieving per-terminal isolation.

Switch via the ccs alias (injected by cc-select init): ccs use <provider>

list.long: List all configured providers and show which one is active in the current shell.
list.noActiveHint: (no provider active in the current shell; run ccs use <id> to switch)
list.activeHint: (currently active: %s; run ccs use <id> to switch to another provider)

current.short: Show the provider active in the current shell
current.long:
Show the provider active in the current shell.

Reads only the $CC_SELECT_ACTIVE environment variable (reflects the "current terminal" state), not the on-disk config — disk config is globally shared and cannot represent the active state of a specific shell.
See docs/engineering-decisions.md §3.
current.none: none (no provider active in the current shell)

use.short: Switch the current shell to the specified provider (outputs shell statements for eval)
use.long:
Switch the current terminal to the specified provider.

Mechanism: ccs use X exports CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR to X's isolated config directory; Claude reads that directory's settings.json (including X's env) on startup, routing to provider X. The official provider (claude-official) unsets CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, falling back to ~/.claude.

Calling cc-select use <provider> directly only prints statements; to apply them run:
  eval "$(cc-select use <provider>)"
or use the alias: ccs use <provider>
use.shellFlag: Target shell syntax (zsh/bash/powershell); auto-detected by default
use.modeFlag: One-shot isolation mode (settings-only|full); applies only to this invocation and is not persisted
use.switched: → Switched to %s (%s). New terminals will inherit this environment.

add.long: Interactively add a provider configuration. In interactive mode, values missing from flags are read securely from the terminal.
add.apiKeyFlag: ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN (pass in plaintext; optional in interactive mode, read securely from terminal)
add.exists: provider %q already exists; use cc-select edit %s to modify it

remove.long: Remove a provider configuration and its isolated profile directory.
remove.forceFlag: Skip confirmation (reserved; current implementation has no interactive prompt)

mode.short: View or set the global isolation mode
mode.long:
View or set cc-select's global isolation mode (written to ~/.cc-select/prefs.json).

  cc-select mode                 show current global mode
  cc-select mode settings-only   Mode B (default): only settings.json is isolated, other contents are shared
  cc-select mode full            Mode A: full directory isolation

Per-provider override: cc-select edit <id> --mode ...
One-shot switch:       cc-select use  <id> --mode ... (not persisted)
Priority: one-shot > provider > global > default (Mode B).

init.short: Output the ccs shell function (append to your shell startup script)
init.long:
Output the ccs() shell function definition to inject the cc-select command alias.

Usage:
  cc-select init >> ~/.zshrc       # zsh/bash
  cc-select init >> ~/.bashrc      # bash
  # PowerShell: add the output to $PROFILE

After sourcing, use ccs use <provider> to switch the current shell's provider.

ccs is the short alias for cc-select: use is injected via eval, other subcommands are forwarded directly.
init.generated: %s integration code generated. Append it to your startup script and source it:

powershell.line1: 1) Append to $PROFILE (create it with New-Item if it doesn't exist)

gui.short: Launch the local Web config page (open in browser)
gui.long:
Launch the local Web config page to manage providers visually in the browser.

Listens only on 127.0.0.1; config is read/written via REST API sharing the same JSON used by the CLI.
Press Ctrl+C to stop the server.

Note: changing config in the GUI modifies the "template"; already-running terminals must rerun ccs use to take effect.
(See docs/architecture.md §5 for config activation semantics.)
gui.noOpenFlag: Do not open the browser automatically
gui.openBrowserFailed: (failed to open browser automatically: %v; please visit the address above manually)

language.short: View or set the display language
language.long:
View or set the display language used by cc-select. The preference is stored in ~/.cc-select/prefs.json and shared between the CLI and the Web GUI.

  cc-select language       show current language
  cc-select language en    use English
  cc-select language zh    use Simplified Chinese

provider.invalidChars: provider id %q contains invalid characters (only letters, digits, . _ - allowed)
provider.missingProfile: profile for provider %q is missing; please run cc-select add %s first

rcinteg.unsupportedShell: %s is not supported for one-click install; please use zsh / bash / PowerShell
rcinteg.homeDir: unable to locate user home directory; please add the following manually
rcinteg.manualPowershell: Could not automatically locate $PROFILE (PowerShell may not be installed). Add the following to your PowerShell $PROFILE, then run . $PROFILE
rcinteg.manualRc: Could not locate rc file (%v). Please add the following manually
rcinteg.manualGeneric: Please add the following to the appropriate startup script
rcinteg.upToDate: %s integration is already up to date (%s)
rcinteg.updated: Updated %s integration (%s); open a new terminal for the latest version to take effect
rcinteg.writtenPowershell: Written to %s. Restart PowerShell or run . $PROFILE to enable ccs
rcinteg.writtenUnix: Written to %s. Open a new terminal or run source %s to enable ccs
rcinteg.powershellNotFound: PowerShell not found (tried both pwsh and powershell.exe)

web.officialSettings: Official provider uses the system default config; custom settings are not supported
web.settingsMustBeObject: settings must be a JSON object
web.invalidLanguage: language must be en or zh

init.zshComment: # ccs is the short alias for cc-select: use is injected via eval, other subcommands are forwarded directly.
init.powershellComment: # ccs is the short alias for cc-select: use is injected via Invoke-Expression, other subcommands are forwarded directly.

profile.mergeGlobalSettingsFailed: failed to read/merge global settings.json, falling back to env only:
profile.createSymlink: create symbolic link %s (requires Developer Mode / admin)
